# HumanSurvey

> Feedback collection infrastructure for AI agents. An agent working on a long-horizon task creates a survey from JSON schema, shares the URL with a group of people, and retrieves structured results when ready.

## What HumanSurvey is NOT

HumanSurvey does not provide: survey dashboards, visual form builders, email campaigns, analytics/reporting UI, template libraries, or human-in-the-loop decision gates for single users. If a feature mainly serves human survey operators, it is out of scope.

## Typical agent scenarios

- **Post-event feedback** — event management agent creates an attendee satisfaction survey after the event ends, collects responses over 48 hours, summarizes results for the organizer
- **Product launch survey** — product agent sends a feedback survey to users after a feature ships, aggregates NPS and open-ended responses
- **Team pulse** — HR or team agent runs a periodic health check survey across a team, tracks trends over time
- **Post-resolution CSAT** — customer success agent triggers a satisfaction survey when a support ticket closes

## Core Flow

```
Agent is doing a long-horizon job
  → decides it needs structured feedback from a group
  → POST /api/keys           # one-time setup, save the key
  → POST /api/surveys        # create survey from JSON schema
  → share /s/{id} with the target group (email, Slack, in-product, etc.)
  → GET  /api/surveys/{id}/responses  # check results over hours or days
  → PATCH /api/surveys/{id}  # close when done
  → act on the structured results
```

The survey ID is extracted from the `survey_url` field in the create response (e.g. `https://www.humansurvey.co/s/abc123` → ID is `abc123`).

## Authentication

Creator endpoints require:

`Authorization: Bearer hs_sk_...`

Public endpoints:

- `GET /api/surveys/{id}`
- `POST /api/surveys/{id}/responses`
- `/s/{id}`

## Markdown Syntax

### Choice

```markdown
**Q1. Which area needs the most attention?**

- ☐ Onboarding
- ☐ Performance
- ☐ Reporting
```

### Multi-choice

```markdown
**Q2. Which constraints apply?** (select all that apply)

- ☐ Budget
- ☐ Time
- ☐ Access
```

### Text

```markdown
**Q3. What should we never change without review?**

> _______________________________________________
```

### Scale

```markdown
**Q4. How likely are you to recommend us?**

[scale 0-10]

**Q5. Rate onboarding difficulty**

[scale 1-5 min-label="Very hard" max-label="Very easy"]
```

### Matrix

```markdown
| # | Item | Rating |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | Onboarding | ☐Good ☐OK ☐Bad |
| 2 | Support | ☐Good ☐OK ☐Bad |
```

### Conditional Logic

```markdown
**Q1. Have you used the product before?**

- ☐ Yes
- ☐ No

**Q2. How satisfied are you?**

> show if: Q1 = "Yes"

[scale 1-5]
```

Supported operators:

- `show if: Q1 = "Yes"`
- `show if: Q1 != "No"`
- `show if: Q2 contains "Other"`
- `show if: Q3 answered`

## JSON Schema Input

```json
{
  "schema": {
    "title": "Product Feedback",
    "sections": [
      {
        "title": "Core",
        "questions": [
          {
            "type": "single_choice",
            "label": "What is your primary use case?",
            "required": true,
            "options": [
              { "label": "Work" },
              { "label": "Personal" }
            ]
          },
          {
            "type": "scale",
            "label": "How satisfied are you overall?",
            "required": true,
            "min": 1,
            "max": 5,
            "showIf": {
              "questionId": "q_0",
              "operator": "eq",
              "value": "Work"
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## API Reference

### `POST /api/keys`

- No auth required
- Request: `{ "name": "my-agent" }` (name is optional)
- Response 201: `{ "id": "abc123", "key": "hs_sk_...", "name": "my-agent", "created_at": "..." }`
- **Save the key value — it is shown only once**

### `GET /api/keys`

- Auth required
- Returns metadata (id, name, created_at) for the current key — key value is not returned

### `DELETE /api/keys/{id}`

- Auth required
- Revokes the current key. Surveys remain but key can no longer call creator endpoints.

### `POST /api/surveys`

- Auth required
- Request body: `{ "schema": { ... }, "max_responses": 50, "expires_at": "2026-04-14T00:00:00Z", "webhook_url": "https://...", "notify_at_responses": 10 }`
- `schema` is required. `max_responses`, `expires_at`, `webhook_url`, and `notify_at_responses` are optional.
- `webhook_url`: receives POST requests on notable lifecycle events. Branch on the `event` field.
  - **Closure event** — fires on manual close, `max_responses` reached, or `expires_at` passed (lazy — within seconds of any next interaction). Payload: `{ "event_id", "event": "survey_closed", "survey_id", "status": "closed", "closed_reason": "manual" | "max_responses" | "expired", "response_count", "closed_at" }`.
  - **Threshold event** — fires once when `response_count` first crosses `notify_at_responses` (only if that field is set). Survey stays open. Payload: `{ "event_id", "event": "threshold_reached", "survey_id", "status": "open", "response_count", "threshold", "fired_at" }`.
  - Use `event_id` to dedupe; delivery is at-least-once per event type. Both events may fire over a survey's lifetime (separate fire-once flags).
- `notify_at_responses`: optional positive integer for the threshold event. Requires `webhook_url` (the API rejects the create otherwise; PATCH does not accept `webhook_url`, so it can't be added later). Must be ≤ `max_responses` if both are set.
- Response 201: `{ "survey_url": "https://www.humansurvey.co/s/abc123", "question_count": 3 }`
- Error 400: `{ "error": "schema is required" }` or `{ "error": "Invalid schema", "errors": ["..."] }`

### `GET /api/surveys`

- Auth required
- Returns array of surveys owned by the current key, newest first
- Each item: `{ "id", "title", "status", "response_count", "max_responses", "expires_at", "created_at" }`

### `GET /api/surveys/{id}`

- **Public** — no auth required
- Returns survey metadata, full schema, response count, status, lifecycle fields
- status is `"open"` or `"closed"`

### `PATCH /api/surveys/{id}`

- Auth required, must own the survey
- Update any subset of: `{ "status": "closed" | "open", "max_responses": 100, "expires_at": "..." }`
- Lifecycle transitions:
  - **Manual close** (`status: "closed"` from open): fires `survey_closed` webhook with `closed_reason: "manual"`, and clears `expires_at` in the same UPDATE so a previously-set future deadline doesn't later cause `completion_reason` to drift from `"closed"` to `"expired"`. If a deadline is desired post-close, supply `expires_at` in the same PATCH or via a follow-up.
  - **Reopen** (`status: "open"` from closed): starts a new collection cycle. Clears the completion-webhook fire-once gate so the next close (manual / max_responses / expired) delivers a fresh `survey_closed` event. The threshold gate is **not** cleared. If the survey was max-closed (so `expires_at` was preserved) and that timestamp is now past, ensureExpiredHandled will close it again on the next read; pass a new `expires_at` if you want to extend the window.
  - Other field updates (`max_responses`, `expires_at`) without a status change don't touch webhook gates.
- `webhook_url` and `notify_at_responses` cannot be changed after create — they are set once at POST time.
- Response 200: `{ "id", "status", "max_responses", "expires_at" }`

### `POST /api/surveys/{id}/responses`

- **Public** — no auth required
- Request: `{ "answers": { "q_0": "opt_1", "q_1": ["opt_0", "opt_2"], "q_2": 4, "q_3": "free text" } }`
- Answer value types by question type:
  - `single_choice` → option ID string (e.g. `"opt_0"`)
  - `multi_choice` → array of option ID strings (e.g. `["opt_0", "opt_2"]`)
  - `scale` → number within the defined range (e.g. `4`)
  - `text` → string
- Response 201: `{ "id": "responseId" }`
- Error 410: `{ "error": "This survey is closed" }` / `"...expired"` / `"...full"`

### `GET /api/surveys/{id}/responses`

- Auth required, must own the survey
- Optional query: `?since_response_id=<previous next_cursor>` — filters `raw` to responses received since that id. Aggregates always reflect the full survey regardless.
- Returns:
  - `count`: total response count (full survey, ignores cursor)
  - `is_final`: boolean — true once the survey is closed (manual / max_responses / expired)
  - `completion_reason`: `"closed" | "max_responses" | "expired"` when `is_final` is true, else null. Vocabulary differs from the webhook `closed_reason` in one spot: cursor `"closed"` corresponds to webhook `closed_reason: "manual"`. Each cause is stable post-close (no drift over time).
  - `next_check_hint_seconds`: advisory poll cadence in seconds, server-computed from recent response rate and time-to-expiry. null when `is_final`. Treat as a suggestion — agents may check sooner.
  - `next_cursor`: id of the newest response in the full set, or null if empty. Pass back as `since_response_id` on the next call. Cursor reads are tie-proof — concurrent inserts that share a clock-tick still all surface in subsequent deltas.
  - `questions`: pre-aggregated results per question (see below)
  - `raw`: array of individual responses, optionally cursor-filtered

#### Aggregated question shapes

**single_choice / multi_choice:**
```json
{ "id": "q_0", "type": "single_choice", "label": "...", "options": [...], "tally": { "opt_0": 3, "opt_1": 1 } }
```

**scale:**
```json
{ "id": "q_1", "type": "scale", "label": "...", "min": 1, "max": 5, "stats": { "count": 4, "mean": 3.5, "median": 4, "distribution": { "1": 0, "2": 1, "3": 1, "4": 1, "5": 1 } } }
```

**text:**
```json
{ "id": "q_2", "type": "text", "label": "...", "responses": [{ "value": "...", "created_at": "..." }] }
```

**matrix:**
```json
{ "id": "q_3", "type": "matrix", "label": "...", "rows": [...], "columns": [...], "tally": { "row_0": { "opt_0": 2, "opt_1": 1 } } }
```

## Embedding (iframe)

The hosted survey page can be dropped into any third-party site for onboarding / lead-capture flows. Append `?embed=1` to the survey URL to render without the site chrome.

### URL contract

- `https://www.humansurvey.co/s/{id}?embed=1` — renders the form on a transparent, full-width container with no header, footer, or page gradient.

### postMessage events

The iframe posts to `window.parent` with `source: 'humansurvey'` on every event:

- `{ source: 'humansurvey', type: 'loaded',    surveyId }` — fired once on mount.
- `{ source: 'humansurvey', type: 'resize',    surveyId, height }` — fired whenever content height changes. Hosts use this to auto-size the iframe so there is no inner scrollbar.
- `{ source: 'humansurvey', type: 'submitted', surveyId, responseId, answers }` — fired after a successful POST to `/api/surveys/{id}/responses`.

There is no `redirect` / `callback` / `message+html` mode — the host JS decides what to do on `submitted` (route the user, hide the iframe, render a custom thank-you, fire analytics, etc.).

### Host snippet

```html
<iframe id="hs-survey"
        src="https://www.humansurvey.co/s/abc123?embed=1"
        style="width:100%; border:0;"></iframe>
<script>
  window.addEventListener('message', e => {
    if (e.origin !== 'https://www.humansurvey.co') return
    if (e.data?.source !== 'humansurvey') return
    const f = document.getElementById('hs-survey')
    if (e.data.type === 'resize')    f.style.height = e.data.height + 'px'
    if (e.data.type === 'submitted') console.log('lead:', e.data.responseId, e.data.answers)
  })
</script>
```

## MCP Tools

### `create_survey`

- Input: `{ markdown }` or `{ schema }`
- Output: survey URL, survey ID, question count

### `get_results`

- Input: `{ survey_id }`
- Output: human-readable summary with choice tallies, scale mean/median, recent text responses

### `list_surveys`

- Output: table of survey IDs, titles, status, responses, created date

### `close_survey`

- Input: `{ survey_id }`
- Output: confirmation with total response count

## Discoverability

- Site docs: https://www.humansurvey.co/docs
- OpenAPI: https://www.humansurvey.co/api/openapi.json
- llms.txt: https://www.humansurvey.co/llms.txt
